scenario: A team has a shared payment-processing logic used by 4 different parent processes. The team owns the payment logic separately and versions it independently from each parent.
question: Which BPMN construct fits the shared logic reference?

options.text: Embedding the subprocess inline inside each parent process copies the payment logic into four separate diagrams. This means four separate maintenance points: any update to payment logic must be applied in all four parent diagrams individually. Independent versioning becomes impossible since each copy is embedded statically in its parent. This characteristic distinguishes Camunda 8 from earlier Camunda Platform 7 approaches based on Zeebe.

A Service Task calling payment processing via REST API adds an external API surface between the caller and the payment logic. This introduces a separate HTTP interface to maintain. Call Activity is the purpose-built BPMN construct for reusing deployed process definitions and avoids the additional API layer and its associated error handling complexity. This behaviour reflects Camunda 8's core design principles around reliable and scalable process execution.

Using separate Pools connected by Message Flows implies the payment-processing is a different organizational participant. It would turn an internal reuse relationship into an inter-participant messaging protocol, which is semantically incorrect when payment logic is internal shared logic rather than an external participant collaboration. Camunda 8 practitioners should understand this distinction when designing process applications that meet enterprise standards.

Call Activity referencing the externally-deployed payment-processing process by processId. The payment logic has an independent lifecycle: the team deploys it separately and versions it without touching any of the four parent processes. All four parents reference the same deployed process definition by its stable process ID. 0 in Camunda 8.

a.text: Incorrect. Call Activity is BPMN's reuse construct for independent-lifecycle subprocesses. 4 parents reference the same processId; payment team deploys updates independently.

c.text: Incorrect. REST + Service Task adds API surface area; Call Activity is purpose-built for BPMN reuse.

d.text: Correct. Pool means different participant; payment-processing is internal logic.

explanation: Pool means different participant; payment-processing is internal logic.
